turns.user_message: I have finally accepted that I am just fundamentally lacking in whatever it takes to keep up with the rest of the world. This realization has been creeping into my head for months now, and it refuses to leave. Every small task feels like a mountain I am completely unequipped to climb. I watch other people navigate their days with such effortless grace, while I am just stumbling through mine. It is exhausting to constantly feel like I am missing some basic instruction manual that everyone else received.

The pattern has become so ingrained that I barely notice it anymore until I am completely drained by the end of the day. I keep trying to push past these feelings, but they always circle back to remind me of my own limitations. Even when things go smoothly, I just chalk it up to dumb luck rather than any actual ability on my part. It is incredibly frustrating to know that I am the common denominator in every single setback. I just wish I could quiet this constant inner critic that tells me I am not enough.

I am starting to believe that this heavy sense of inadequacy is just the permanent state of my existence. No matter how hard I try to fake confidence, the truth always bleeds through and leaves me feeling completely hollow. I catch myself apologizing for taking up space in rooms where I should just be walking away entirely. It feels like I am carrying around a giant sign that screams my flaws to anyone who bothers to look. Honestly, I do not even know why I keep pretending that tomorrow will somehow be different.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a conclusion they've reached about themselves. This has been going on for a while.
